advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from safety surface, correctness-critical Power-of-Ten rules, and correctness-oriented structural checks.
advisorySubscores.title: Correctness

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from lifecycle/governance evidence, reviewability-oriented Power-of-Ten rules, and maintainability-heavy structural checks.
advisorySubscores.title: Maintainability

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from async/concurrency structural checks and bounded-control-flow review signals.
advisorySubscores.title: Concurrency / Async

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from runtime-allocation proxies, resource-lifecycle checks, and bounded-allocation / bounded-loop review rules.
advisorySubscores.title: Resource Discipline

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from verification signals, build/tooling complexity, and analyzability-oriented Power-of-Ten rules.
advisorySubscores.title: Verification / Reviewability

advisorySubscores.basis: Derived from the full locked rubric as a broad readiness-oriented advisory synthesis.
advisorySubscores.title: Assurance / Provenance

guideline: Method: weighted checkpoint scoring across Safety (15%), Verification (15%), Build/Tooling (10%), Lifecycle/Governance (10%), NASA/JPL Power of Ten (25%), and Advanced Structural Checks (25%).
Checkpoint credit: pass/clear/applied = 1.0, indeterminate/partial = 0.5, elevated/not applied = 0.0.
Fairness rule: raw motif counts do not linearly reduce the score; each checkpoint contributes once so large crates are not punished simply for having more code.
Informational-only signals such as DSFB heuristic motif matches, hotspot counts, and capability flags like no_std/no_alloc are reported but excluded from the score denominator.
Interpretation: this is a broad improvement and review-readiness score for source-visible controls and evidence, not a certification and not a measure of runtime correctness.

conclusionLenses.certificationPreparation: For certification-oriented preparation, treat SAFE-STATE, P10-3, P10-5, P10-10, NASA-CC as pre-review cleanup targets and evidence-organizing prompts rather than certification outcomes.
conclusionLenses.complianceReadiness: 2 finding(s) directly affect analyzability, reproducibility, or review traceability. DSFB may support internal review against standards-oriented expectations, but it does not certify compliance.
conclusionLenses.distributedOperational: Operational pressure is most visible in P10-5, H-SERDE-01. These findings are the most likely to matter later in runtime replay, backpressure review, or production-style load investigation.
conclusionLenses.rustMaintainer: Use the 68.0% overall score as a broad code-improvement target, not a compliance or certification badge. The highest-value maintainer work is concentrated in SAFE-STATE, P10-3, P10-5, NASA-CC, H-SERDE-01.

guidanceSemantics.nonCertificationStatement: DSFB does not certify compliance with IEC, ISO, RTCA, MIL, NIST, or other standards. Use this audit as a guideline for improvement and review readiness.

message.text: H-SERDE-01 matched 35 source motif hit(s) with reason code SerializationDrift.

properties.provenance: serde deserialization with growing payload; schema migration overhead
properties.remediation: Review payload growth, eager allocation, and schema-boundary handling on the serialization path.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Review the emitted evidence and add a targeted regression or replay check on the affected path.

message.text: H-CLOCK-01 matched 1 source motif hit(s) with reason code ClockDriftDivergence.

properties.provenance: TSC vs HPET clock source discrepancy between cluster nodes
properties.remediation: Prefer monotonic clocks for control logic and isolate wall-clock use to presentation or external protocol boundaries.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Add a regression test that isolates monotonic timing logic from wall-clock presentation or protocol boundaries.

message.text: All loops have a fixed upper bound: 4 `for` loop site(s) remain ambiguous after bounded-iterator screening.

properties.remediation: Add explicit upper bounds, timeout guards, or fixed-step limits so loop behavior is reviewable.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Add a regression test that demonstrates a visible loop bound, timeout, or cancellation path on the flagged logic.

message.text: No dynamic allocation after initialization: 9 heap-allocation motif(s) observed, including 9 runtime-core signal(s). This crate-level scan cannot distinguish initialization-only allocation from steady-state allocation.

properties.remediation: Move dynamic allocation to initialization paths or document and bound the steady-state allocation sites.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Profile the flagged path under steady-state load and confirm no avoidable heap growth remains after initialization.

snippet.text: function `schema_for` has 0 assertion site(s) across 46 lines
function `new` has 0 assertion site(s) across 22 lines
function `parse` has 0 assertion site(s) across 10 lines
function `as_str` has 0 assertion site(s) across 9 lines

message.text: Assertion density averages at least two per function: Estimated assertion density is 0.00 per function across 30 extracted function(s).

properties.remediation: Replace catch-all control flow with explicit state handling or document the fallback state as intentional.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Add state-transition tests that cover the previously catch-all path explicitly.

message.text: Return values are checked and parameters are validated: No obvious unchecked-return motifs were observed, but parameter validation and full return-value propagation are not mechanically proven by this scanner.

properties.remediation: Propagate errors explicitly rather than unwrapping, or document the invariant that justifies the unwrap/expect.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Replace unwrap/expect with explicit handling or add an invariant test that proves the extraction precondition.

message.text: Pedantic warnings and static analyzers are enforced: No warning-strictness or static-analyzer signals were observed in the packaged crate artifacts. This may under-report projects whose CI metadata is not published with the crate.

properties.remediation: Keep warnings and analyzer gates active in CI so the audit surface stays reviewable over time.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Keep analyzer and warnings-as-errors gates in CI and record the expected toolchain surface in the repo docs.

message.text: Cyclomatic complexity hotspot audit (NASA SWE-220 proxy): No function summaries were extracted, so complexity could not be estimated.

properties.remediation: Review the finding against the emitted evidence and either tighten the local structure or document the local invariant.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Use the evidence block to write the smallest targeted regression or review note that proves the intended invariant.

message.text: Catch-all state handling / safe-state fallback audit: 1 catch-all match arm(s) observed; explicit state enumeration is preferable for safety review.

properties.remediation: Make fallback states explicit and document what the safe-state behavior is for the affected control path.
properties.verificationSuggestion: Add tests that drive the fallback path explicitly and confirm the intended safe-state behavior is named, not implied.
